name: bun:sqlite Synchronous Pattern
description: bun:sqlite is synchronous. Use Database class from 'bun:sqlite'. Enable WAL mode: db.exec('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL'). Set busy_timeout: db.exec('PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000'). Use db.prepare() for prepared statements. db.query().all() for SELECT, db.run() for INSERT/UPDATE. No async/await needed for DB ops.

content: All JSON files written via Bun.write must include a trailing newline: `${JSON.stringify(data, null, '\t')}\n`. Biome enforces trailing newlines, and omitting them causes lint failures on generated/runtime files like sessions.json. Apply consistently across all write sites — grep for JSON.stringify to find them all.

description: bun:test mock.module() is process-global and leaks across all test files run in the same bun test invocation. Mocking a module like ../src/config.ts in one test file causes other test files that import the same module to get the mocked version, causing 73+ test failures.
resolution: Avoid mock.module entirely; instead extract testable pure functions or use spyOn for instance methods.

name: macOS temp dir symlink resolution for git worktree tests
description: macOS temp dir symlink: /var/folders -> /private/var/folders. Git resolves symlinks in worktree paths, so when creating temp git repos for tests, wrap the path with realpathSync() to avoid path comparison mismatches. This affects listWorktrees output and any path-based lookups like removeWorktree branch resolution.

name: bd CLI JSON normalization
description: bd show --json returns an array (not single object), and uses 'issue_type' field name instead of 'type'. Client must unwrap array for show() and map issue_type->type via normalizeIssue(). Integration tests exposed these mismatches that mocks hid.

name: Bun test.skipIf needs synchronous check
description: bun:test test.skipIf(condition) evaluates condition at test registration time (synchronously), not at runtime. Use Bun.spawnSync for CLI availability checks, not Bun.spawn in beforeAll. beforeAll runs after test registration, so async availability checks are too late for skipIf.

name: Selective Bun.spawn spy for testing
description: Save original Bun.spawn before spying, define a selectiveMock function with (...args: unknown[]): unknown signature that branches on cmd[0], then cast with mockImplementation(selectiveMock as typeof Bun.spawn). This avoids TypeScript overload errors while allowing real subprocess calls to pass through.

name: Delete/modify conflicts for testing tier escalation
description: To test resolver tiers beyond auto-resolve with real git: create a delete/modify conflict (delete file on one branch, modify on other). Git reports it as unmerged but produces NO conflict markers in the working copy, causing resolveConflictsKeepIncoming to return null. This naturally escalates past Tier 2. For Tier 4 (reimagine), use a separate file in entry.filesModified that exists on both branches, since git show fails for deleted files.

name: Template clone for test repos
description: Cache a template git repo on first call (lazy singleton), then use git clone for subsequent repos (1 subprocess instead of 5). Avoid --local flag on macOS — hardlinks trigger EFAULT in Bun rm(). Use GIT_AUTHOR/COMMITTER env vars instead of per-repo git config to eliminate 2 more subprocess spawns.
git init without -b main causes branch name mismatches between macOS (main) and Linux CI (master).
resolution: Always pass -b main to git init in test helpers to ensure consistent branch naming across platforms.

name: test-infinite-loop-isolation
description: When testing commands that start infinite loops (like dashboard polling), use process.chdir to a temp dir WITHOUT required config files so initialization throws BEFORE the loop starts. This proves validation passed while preventing background loops from leaking across test files. Promise.race abandons promises but doesn't stop running code.
Dashboard tests starting infinite poll loops via Promise.race leak loops after test completes. Abandoned promise's loop continues writing to stdout, polluting subsequent test files.
resolution: chdir to a temp dir without .overstory/config.yaml so loadConfig() throws before entering the while loop. Prevents infinite loop from ever starting.

name: ci-git-identity-config
description: CI test repos need repo-level git config (user.name/email) because resolver's runGit doesn't pass GIT_TEST_ENV and CI runners lack global git identity. Fix: set config in createTempGitRepo().

name: bun-spy-mock-clear
description: bun v1.3.9+ test reporter may flush output through console.log/error between spy creation and test action, inflating call counts. Always call mockClear() after spyOn() before the assertion-triggering action.

'Never mock what you can use for real' has a critical exception: tests calling killAllTmuxSessions() or other global-state-mutating functions MUST mock those operations. Real tmux kills during bun test destroyed a live agent swarm.
resolution: The philosophy applies to isolated resources (temp dirs, :memory: DBs) but NOT to shared global resources (tmux server, process table). Add tmux mocking to clean.test.ts.

name: pathExists helper
description: Use stat() from node:fs/promises for both files and directories. Bun.file().exists() only works for files, not directories. Pattern: async function pathExists(path: string): Promise<boolean> { try { await stat(path); return true; } catch { return false; } }

name: Path normalization for symlinks
description: When comparing file paths from different sources (git worktree list, SessionStore, file system), use realpathSync() to normalize symlinks before comparison. On macOS, /tmp -> /private/tmp causes mismatches without normalization. Wrap in try/catch since realpathSync throws for non-existent paths.

content: Test git repo setup: Always disable GPG signing in test git repos with 'git config commit.gpgsign false' to prevent test failures from GPG configuration.

Mail test pattern for repeated checks: When testing mail check multiple times, must send new messages between checks since check/checkInject mark messages as read. Use separate client.send() calls to create fresh unread messages for each test assertion.

name: Dependency Injection for Test Isolation
description: Instead of mock.module() (which leaks across tests), define optional deps parameter on functions and pass mock implementations. Example: checkConsistency(config, dir, deps?) where deps = {listSessions, isProcessAlive}. Tests create mocks and pass via deps param. This prevents module-level mock leakage.

agents.test.ts had process.chdir(tempDir) in beforeEach but never restored originalCwd in afterEach, leaving the Bun test process with a deleted temp dir as cwd. This poisoned subsequent test files in the same worker — 6 tests in prime.test.ts and color.test.ts failed only in CI due to test file ordering.
resolution: Always save and restore originalCwd when using process.chdir in tests. Subprocess tests using cwd: process.cwd() with relative imports are fragile — use absolute paths derived from import.meta.dir instead. These bugs are invisible locally since they depend on Bun test file ordering.

content: When adding a required field to OverstoryConfig, ALL test fixtures across the codebase (src/agents/manifest.test.ts, src/doctor/*.test.ts) must be updated to include the new field — this is a cross-cutting concern that affects files outside any single builder's scope. Consider making new OverstoryConfig fields optional or assigning fixture updates to a separate task when doing batch coordination.
Biome cannot lint files in .overstory/worktrees/* due to the !**/.overstory exclusion in biome.json. This is expected — run tsc typecheck and bun test as the quality gates for worktree agents.

AgentRuntime interface extension pattern: add new capabilities as optional fields (readonly headless?, buildDirectSpawn?, parseEvents?) to keep backward compatibility with all existing runtime adapters. New supporting types (DirectSpawnOpts, AgentEvent) go in the same file before the interface definition, under their own section comment.

SaplingRuntime test pattern: use real temp dirs (mkdtemp), deployConfig calls write both SAPLING.md and .sapling/guards.json, guards are parsed via JSON.parse for assertion, parseTranscript uses first-model-wins (!model guard) unlike Codex which uses last-model-wins. biome check must run from canonical repo root (npx biome check <worktree-path>) not via bun run lint in worktrees.

SaplingRuntime model alias fix pattern (overstory-28ae): buildEnv must forward ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL env vars from model.env so buildDirectSpawn can resolve gateway-routed aliases. Add loop: for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(providerEnv)) { if (key.startsWith('ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_') && key.endsWith('_MODEL')) env[key] = value; }. buildDirectSpawn fallback map (SAPLING_ALIAS_FALLBACKS) resolves bare aliases (haiku/sonnet/opus) to concrete model IDs when no gateway env var is present.

DirectSpawnOpts.model optional pattern (overstory-7110): making model?: string optional in DirectSpawnOpts allows runtimes to conditionally include --model. When undefined, runtime omits --model so agent uses its own config. Safe for runtimes that have their own model config (like sapling). Existing toEqual tests on ResolvedModel need isExplicitOverride added when field is included in all return paths.

Optional field roundtrip pattern for SQLite: when adding an optional field (promptVersion?: string | null) to an interface that roundtrips through SQLite, use conditional spread in rowToSession: '...(row.col \!== null ? { fieldName: row.col } : {})'. This preserves undefined semantics for toEqual() tests — stores that don't set the field get undefined (not null) back, matching original objects.

schema-consistency.test.ts must be updated when adding SQLite columns (overstory-bdbe): src/schema-consistency.test.ts has hardcoded expected column lists for each table. When adding a column to sessions/store.ts CREATE_TABLE, you must also add the column name to the expected array in schema-consistency.test.ts. This file is typically not in builder scope — request scope extension from parent.

PROFILE_INSTRUCTIONS placeholder pattern (overstory-1736): OverlayConfig.profileContent?: string field holds Layer 2 (deployment profile) content. formatProfile() in overlay.ts is a pass-through — the caller (canopy) owns formatting. {{PROFILE_INSTRUCTIONS}} placeholder in overlay.md.tmpl sits between {{BASE_DEFINITION}} and the assignment section, creating the three-layer HOW+WHAT_KIND+WHAT system.

Optional field pattern for new OverstoryConfig watchdog fields: when adding new fields to OverstoryConfig.watchdog that have defaults in DEFAULT_CONFIG, declare them as optional (rpcTimeoutMs?: number) rather than required. This avoids breaking the 13+ test fixtures in src/doctor/*.test.ts and src/agents/manifest.test.ts that construct partial watchdog objects inline. DEFAULT_CONFIG ensures runtime paths always have populated values; validateConfig guards checks with !== undefined.

description: File /Users/jayminwest/Projects/os-eco/overstory/.overstory/worktrees/watchdog-format-fixer/src/watchdog/daemon.ts required 3 edits during session — high iteration suggests complexity

content: Bun ReadableStreamReadResult type divergence fix: Bun's ReadableStreamDefaultReadResult<T> is not assignable to the standard ReadableStreamReadResult<T> (value is optional in done result vs required). When typing a Race union that wraps reader.read() results, use 'type ReadResult = Awaited<ReturnType<typeof reader.read>>>' instead of 'ReadableStreamReadResult<Uint8Array>' to avoid TS2322.

name: headless-connection-pid-kill
description: HeadlessClaudeConnection uses process.kill(pid, 0) for getState liveness check and process.kill(pid, 'SIGTERM') + poll loop + SIGKILL escalation for abort(). sigkillDelayMs is constructor-configurable (default 2000ms) for test speed. Structural typing for the stdin parameter avoids circular deps between process.ts and connections.ts.

content: killAgent() prefers conn.abort() when RuntimeConnection is registered, then calls removeConnection(), then returns early. Falls through to PID/tmux kill only when abort() throws. executeEscalationAction must carry getConnection/removeConnection deps for all killAgent() callers.

runDaemonTick liveness check calls conn.getState() first when a RuntimeConnection exists. idle|working → tmuxAlive=true + lastActivity refresh. error → tmuxAlive=false (no refresh). On rejection/timeout → removeConn() + tmux fallback. Without conn, uses tmux.isSessionAlive() + event-based detection for headless agents.
Biome cannot lint files in .overstory/worktrees/* due to the !**/.overstory exclusion in biome.json — use 'bunx biome check <file>' directly to verify formatting in worktrees, not 'bun run lint'

killAgent() uses conn.abort() when RuntimeConnection is registered, then falls back to PID/tmux. In killAgent(), call getConn(session.agentName) first; if present, await conn.abort() and return — avoids branching on isHeadless. DI: _getConnection added to killAgent ctx, default-initialized from options._getConnection ?? getConnection.

runDaemonTick liveness check uses conn.getState() first when RuntimeConnection exists for both headless AND tmux agents. status 'idle'|'working' → tmuxAlive=true + lastActivity refresh. status 'error' → tmuxAlive=false (no refresh). On rejection/timeout → removeConn() + tmux fallback. Without conn, headless falls back to proc.isAlive(pid), tmux falls back to tmux.isSessionAlive(). No branch on isHeadless needed.

name: biome-modal-backdrop
description: Operator-console mail composer modal: backdrop is a real <button> element, not a div with onClick — biome's noStaticElementInteractions rejects interactive divs. The dialog wrapper uses role='dialog' aria-modal='true', the backdrop is an absolutely-positioned full-bleed <button type='button'> calling onClose, and the Card sits relative on top. Conditional inputs inside <label> trip noLabelWithoutControl; use htmlFor + id + a single <input> with conditional readOnly instead.

content: ui/src/routes/mail/api.ts wraps every fetch in the {success, data, error} envelope; new endpoints (sendMail/replyMail/deleteMail) follow the same shape: throw new Error(json.error ?? 'X failed') on !success, return json.data otherwise. POSTs send Content-Type: application/json; query invalidation goes through queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey: ['mail']}).

name: DOM-stable optimistic→real bubble keying
description: For chat bubbles that transition from pending to real, key the React element on a stable clientToken (uuid) generated at submit time rather than the eventual server message id. The list item carries id='pending-<token>' until the real id lands; the bubble's React key stays the clientToken throughout, so React reconciles state in place and the DOM node never unmounts. Reference: ui/src/routes/coordinator/Thread.tsx (turn.pending?.clientToken ?? turn.id) and ConsolePage.tsx pending lifecycle.

content: Biome auto-format via 'bun x biome check --write .' walks the entire UI tree from the cwd; when invoked from inside a worktree it can touch files outside your declared file scope (e.g. unrelated export-order normalization in components/ui/*). Always git diff after auto-format and 'git checkout HEAD --' any out-of-scope changes before committing. Confirmed: resizable.tsx export reorder leaked through during overstory-82b4.

UI worktrees do not auto-install dependencies on creation. Before running 'bun run typecheck' or 'bun run lint' inside .overstory/worktrees/<name>/ui, run 'bun install' at both the worktree root and the ui/ subdir or you'll get a flood of TS2307 'Cannot find module react' errors that look like real failures. Confirmed in overstory-82b4 worktree.

name: shadcn-hand-vendor-bun
description: Hand-vendor shadcn primitives in this repo: copy from shadcn registry, replace Radix primitive imports, use cn() from @/lib/utils, set data-slot="<name>" on root, alphabetize exports for biome organizeImports. Skip the shadcn CLI entirely (it stalls in non-tty agent contexts per mx-2acfd3/mx-b9551e).

content: Pin @biomejs/biome to an exact version (no ^) in ui/package.json. The biome.json  URL is version-specific, so a caret-range that resolves to a newer minor on fresh install introduces stricter assist rules and breaks lint on pre-existing files outside any single agent's scope.

name: bun-test-no-dom-pure-helper
description: Bun's test runner has no DOM globals (no document/window/ResizeObserver/MutationObserver). For React hooks that touch the DOM, extract the decision logic as a pure exported helper (e.g., isNearBottom, computeFadeMask) and unit-test that. Hook-level integration is left to e2e, which keeps bun test fast and dep-free in the worktree root.

name: url-driven-cross-route-state
description: URL-driven cross-route state in React Router 7: use useSearchParams in a layout/parent and pass derived value down as props, while child routes also read the same param. Avoids prop drilling and React context for simple ephemeral state like a 'current run' picker. Use { replace: true } to avoid polluting history.

name: scroll-area-vs-plain-div-when-using-mask-fade-hooks
description: When applying useScrollFade or useAutoScroll to a Radix ScrollArea, the visible scroll element is the inner viewport (data-slot='scroll-area-viewport'), not the Root. Querying that nested element via parent ref and forwarding to a hook works but is fragile. Simpler: drop the ScrollArea wrapper and use a plain <div ref className='flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-auto'>. The mask-image fade in useScrollFade replaces the visual cue ScrollArea provided.

name: dismissable-derived-autocomplete-popover
description: For React autocomplete popovers whose visibility is derived from input value (e.g. isSlashOnly(value)), add a 'dismissed' boolean state and AND it with the derived predicate: showMenu = derived(value) && !dismissed. Reset dismissed=false on textarea onChange so user-typing reopens it; setDismissed(true) in Escape handler, document mousedown click-outside listener (only attached when showMenu), onBlur, submit handler, and selection handler. On menu items use onMouseDown={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} so blur does not fire before click — otherwise selection callback never runs.

name: coordinator-stopped-cta-flow
description: Stopped-coordinator CTA chains POST /api/coordinator/start, polls fetchCoordinatorState() every 500ms with 30s deadline, then POST /api/coordinator/send. Errors stay inline in dialog; on success close dialog and appendSystem('Run started'). Reset newRunError when dialog closes (wrap setNewRunOpen so closing also clears stale error).

content: biome-react-hook-deps-stable-callback: biome's lint/correctness/useExhaustiveDependencies fires on useCallback closures even when the omitted dep is itself a stable useCallback. Add the stable callback to the dep array (no functional difference) instead of disabling the rule.

name: biome-root-check-noop-in-worktree
description: Running 'biome check .' or 'bun x biome check .' from inside a path under .overstory/worktrees/ reports 'Checked 0 files' / 'These paths were provided but ignored: .' because biome.json's '!**/.overstory' exclusion matches absolute paths and the cwd itself contains '.overstory'. UI workspace lint (bun run lint inside ui/) still works because it only walks ui/ and biome resolves relative to that subtree. When verifying cherry-picks in a worktree, treat root biome as N/A; rely on subworkspace lint scripts and the canonical-side biome run.

name: atomic-pid-lock-via-link
description: Atomic file lock via write-temp-then-link (overstory-8ef6): fs.open(path, 'wx') is NOT sufficient for atomic lock acquisition because the file appears at the lock path BEFORE writeFile completes — a racing reader sees the empty file, treats it as corrupted, and reclaims it. Use writeFile(temp) then link(temp, lock) instead: link() is the atomic primitive (fails EEXIST if dst exists) AND the lock path appears with full PID content already present, so racing readers always observe a complete file. See src/utils/pid.ts:acquirePidLock.

name: sqlite-update-clear-on-terminal-state
description: When a SQLite column tracks a transient resource (e.g. tmux_session) tied to lifecycle state, clear it in the same UPDATE statement that lands the terminal state — not via a separate clearX path. updateStateStmt uses a CASE expression on the bound $state param; tryTransitionStmts (per-target prepared statements) inline the clear in the SET clause for terminal targets only. Single-statement clear keeps state and resource-cleanup atomic; CAS rejection cannot half-clear. Discovered while fixing overstory-14c0 (sessions.db tmux_session left stale after completed/zombie transition).

name: ui-route-shim-after-merge
description: After parallel UI dispatch merges back to main, fold the route-local api/ws copies (created to avoid file-collision) back into ui/src/lib/{api,ws}.ts and replace the local copies with thin re-export shims that preserve the original import names. Use 'fetchMailAgents as fetchAgents' aliasing in the shim to dedupe collisions with other lib exports (e.g. fetchAgents AgentRow[]). Remove TODO headers; keep MailMessage shape consistent across lib/api.ts and routes/mail/types.ts (TS structural typing makes them interchangeable so out-of-scope callers via @/routes/mail/types stay compatible).

name: ui-route-shim-removal-after-merge
description: Phase 2 of route-local UI dedup (overstory-e174 redo): once all sibling UI views have merged on main, fully consolidate by deleting the route-local api/ws files (not keeping shims) and updating each consumer to import from @/lib/{api,ws}. When a route-local helper has the same name as a lib export with a different signature (here: fetchAgents string[] vs fetchAgents AgentRow[]), rename the lib helper (fetchMailAgents) and update consumers to the new name rather than aliasing in a shim. Always run quality gates (ui lint+typecheck, root bun test) before AND after rebasing onto current main. Note: serve.test.ts falls-back-to-package-bundled-ui/dist requires 'cd ui && bun run build' first — without ui/dist the local run shows 4270/4271, after build it shows 4271/4271.

name: biome-input-component-not-recognized-by-a11y
description: biome's lint/a11y/noLabelWithoutControl does not see through capitalized custom React components like <Input>/<Select>. When refactoring native <input>/<select> nested inside <label> into a project primitive, the rule starts firing. Resolution: convert label nesting to explicit htmlFor + id with useId-derived strings (matches the project pattern in mail/Composer 'To' label).

name: shadcn-ring-elevation-tokenized
description: Migrate shadcn primitives from shadow-* to ring-1 ring-foreground/{10|15} for elevation. Cards/buttons/dropdowns use /10; modal dialogs use /15 for higher contrast vs overlay. Order classes after border declarations. Ratio-based radius scale (calc(var(--radius)*0.6/0.8/1/1.4)) replaces additive scale so radius scales smoothly when --radius is overridden.

content: shadcn Button/Badge data-variant/data-size attribute pattern: surface cva variant + size as data attributes on the rendered element (data-variant={variant ?? 'default'}, data-size={size ?? 'default'}) alongside data-slot. Coalesce undefined to 'default' since cva defaults are 'default'. Lets consumers hook CSS like [data-slot=button][data-variant=destructive] without parsing className.
